tagline: Achiote-chipotle chicken, charred corn, and a smoky tomatillo finish.
signal_rank_note: Al pastor is an emerging-tier signal in WeHo — solid but not a headliner versus mole or miso in this DMA.
novelty_check: Chipotle has no fixed dish list; this is a specific assembly built around the LTO al pastor protein with a pineapple-forward, smoky build distinct from default chicken/steak bowls.
confidence_reason: Al pastor scores only 0.29 in WeHo, but the LTO protein is in-pantry and pineapple-achiote pairings have proven national pull; locally Mexican-fusion (Gracias Madre) is a stronger frame than al pastor itself.

dish_potential: A Mole Burrito Bowl line — barbacoa or sofritas ladled with real mole over cilantro-lime rice, finished with queso blanco and pickled onion; opens a premium-tier bowl tier above the standard salsa matrix.
operational_lift: Mole ships as a heat-and-hold sauce in a single hotel pan on the line — one new SKU, one ladle station, no new prep skill.
comp_context: Gracias Madre on Melrose is winning the plant-based mole conversation in WeHo, and Guelaguetza (a short drive east) sets the city's mole benchmark — Chipotle has zero presence in this top-signal flavor.

tagline: Braised black lentils, harissa honey chicken, and a cool tahini finish.
signal_rank_note: Lentils are a notable-tier signal in WeHo — meaningful but not top-of-mind versus mole or miso in this neighborhood.
novelty_check: CAVA has no fixed dish list; this build pairs the black lentil base (rarely featured) with harissa honey chicken and a tahini-forward dressing for a distinct lentil-led identity.
confidence_reason: Lentils at 0.33 plus WeHo's plant-forward, Mediterranean-leaning context (Crossroads, Open Sesame) supports the build, but lentils don't yet trend as a hero ingredient locally.

dish_potential: A Roasted Cauliflower + Tahini bowl line — charred cauliflower florets with sumac, lemon herb tahini, and pickled onion; unlocks a true plant-forward hero protein beyond falafel for WeHo's vegan-leaning crowd.
operational_lift: Cauliflower roasts in the same oven cycle as current roasted veg — one SKU, no new equipment, drop-in to the hot well.
comp_context: Crossroads Kitchen and Open Sesame both run charred-cauliflower mezze that consistently land on best-of WeHo lists; CAVA's current 'roasted vegetables' blend buries cauliflower instead of featuring it.

tagline: Miso-glazed salmon, apple kimchi, and crispy rice over wild rice.
signal_rank_note: Miso is a top-tier signal in WeHo — strong demand and well-aligned with the neighborhood's Korean/Japanese openness.
novelty_check: Distinct from Miso Glazed Salmon (which is power greens + spicy broccoli + ginger and skews green-forward) and Crispy Rice Bowl (chicken-led, no salmon, no kimchi) — this is a wild-rice, kimchi-anchored fermented-flavor build.
confidence_reason: Miso 0.81 is the highest deliverable signal for Sweetgreen in WeHo; Moonbowls validates Korean-Japanese hybrid demand and every component is on-line today.

dish_potential: A Burrata + Pesto Summer Plate — burrata over arugula with tomatoes, basil, pesto vinaigrette, and garlic breadcrumbs; opens a premium $18+ tier and a clear evening/date-night occasion Sweetgreen currently misses.
operational_lift: Burrata is a portioned cold-line scoop (single ball per bowl) — no cook step, but requires tight cold-chain and short shelf life, so it's a moderate lift on waste management.
comp_context: WeHo indies on Melrose and Santa Monica Blvd (Jon & Vinny's nearby, plus countless Italian-leaning cafes) have made burrata-on-greens a default expectation; Sweetgreen has no creamy cheese answer beyond feta and goat.
